findings.statement: Cross-provider median H100 rental clears near $2.99/GPU-hour
findings.why: H100 remains the benchmark merchant-GPU rental SKU; the clearing median proxies live end demand for GPU capacity.

impact.mechanism: Rental clearing prices proxy end demand for merchant GPU compute capacity.

evidence.source: AIMultiple Cloud GPU Rental Price Index (cross-provider median)
evidence.excerpt: NVIDIA H100 — 'The cohort median is now around $2.99/GPU-hour'

findings.statement: Cross-provider median B200 rental clears at $6.11/GPU-hour
findings.why: The premium commanded by the newest Blackwell SKU signals strong demand for the latest merchant GPUs.

impact.mechanism: Elevated rental price on the newest flagship reflects demand for latest-generation merchant GPU capacity.

evidence.source: AIMultiple Cloud GPU Rental Price Index (cross-provider median)
evidence.excerpt: NVIDIA B200 — 'B200 median $6.11'

findings.statement: AMD MI300X median rental of $2.72/GPU-hour sits below the H100 median
findings.why: MI300X clears below the H100 median, consistent with AMD's undercut-to-win positioning against the CUDA ecosystem.

impact.mechanism: Competitive AMD rental pricing draws demand toward alternative merchant GPUs.

evidence.source: AIMultiple Cloud GPU Rental Price Index (cross-provider median)
evidence.excerpt: AMD MI300X — 'MI300X median $2.72'

findings.statement: Google Ironwood TPU scales to 9,216 chips delivering 42.5 Exaflops as an inference-optimized merchant-GPU alternative
findings.why: A datacenter-scale custom accelerator purpose-built for inference is a credible substitute that diverts inference workloads away from merchant GPUs.

impact.mechanism: Custom TPU capacity substitutes for merchant GPU demand at hyperscaler scale.

evidence.source: Google Ironwood TPU announcement (blog.google)
evidence.excerpt: 'scales up to 9,216 liquid cooled chips'; '42.5 Exaflops' total compute

findings.statement: Ironwood roughly doubles perf-per-watt versus prior-gen Trillium TPU
findings.why: Improving TPU energy efficiency strengthens the economic case for custom silicon over merchant GPUs in inference fleets.

impact.mechanism: Better perf-per-watt on custom silicon erodes the TCO advantage of merchant GPUs.

evidence.source: Google Ironwood TPU announcement (blog.google)
evidence.excerpt: '2x relative to Trillium, our sixth generation TPU' and 'nearly 30x more power efficient than our first Cloud TPU from 2018'

findings.statement: Intel introduces 'Crescent Island' inference GPU (up to 160GB) with sampling in H2 2026
findings.why: A third credible merchant-GPU vendor targeting inference and agentic AI adds competitive supply, though it is early-stage (sampling only).

impact.mechanism: New inference-focused merchant GPU entrant increases competitive supply against NVIDIA and AMD.

evidence.source: Intel new data-center AI chip / roadmap (Yahoo Finance)
evidence.excerpt: Intel unveiled 'Crescent Island,' a data center GPU using the Xe3P microarchitecture with up to 160GB memory; customer sampling begins in H2 2026.

findings.statement: Meta commits to ~6 GW of AMD Instinct MI450 GPUs in a deal valued up to ~$100 billion
findings.why: A multi-gigawatt, multi-year Meta commitment is a landmark design win that materially lifts AMD merchant-GPU demand.

impact.mechanism: Large hyperscaler design win pulls forward AMD Instinct GPU demand and validates a second merchant-GPU supplier.

evidence.source: Introl blog - Meta-AMD deal vs NVIDIA / custom ASIC landscape (secondary)
evidence.excerpt: Meta-AMD commitment: 6 gigawatts of custom AMD Instinct MI450 GPUs; ~$100 billion potential multi-year deal value

findings.statement: AMD historically prices data-center GPUs 20-30% below NVIDIA equivalents
findings.why: Persistent price undercutting is AMD's lever to offset NVIDIA's CUDA ecosystem advantage and win share.

impact.mechanism: Below-NVIDIA pricing draws price-sensitive demand toward AMD merchant GPUs.

evidence.source: Introl blog - Meta-AMD deal vs NVIDIA / custom ASIC landscape (secondary)
evidence.excerpt: AMD historically 'priced its data center GPUs 20-30% below NVIDIA's equivalent products' to offset the CUDA ecosystem advantage.

findings.statement: Custom AI ASICs projected to capture 15-25% of the overall accelerator market with shipments tripling 2024-2027
findings.why: Hyperscaler custom silicon is structurally displacing a meaningful slice of merchant-GPU demand, a headwind that includes AMD.

impact.mechanism: Custom ASIC substitution reduces the total addressable demand for merchant GPUs.

evidence.source: Introl blog - Meta-AMD deal vs NVIDIA / custom ASIC landscape (secondary)
evidence.excerpt: custom silicon capturing '15-25% of the overall market.'

findings.statement: AMD Q1 2026 revenue of $10.3 billion, up 38% year-over-year
findings.why: Broad-based revenue momentum confirms healthy end demand across AMD's portfolio.

impact.mechanism: Accelerating total revenue signals strengthening demand supportive of the Instinct GPU ramp.

evidence.source: AMD Q1 2026 financial results (AMD Investor Relations)
evidence.excerpt: 'First quarter revenue was $10.3 billion' — up 38% year-over-year (roughly flat quarter-over-quarter).

findings.statement: AMD Data Center segment revenue $5.8 billion, up 57% year-over-year on Instinct GPU ramp
findings.why: Data Center growth is driven directly by the Instinct GPU ramp, the core merchant-GPU demand signal for AMD.

impact.mechanism: Instinct GPU shipment ramp drives Data Center demand growth.

evidence.source: AMD Q1 2026 financial results (AMD Investor Relations)
evidence.excerpt: 'Data Center segment revenue was $5.8 billion, up 57% year-over-year, driven by strong demand for AMD EPYC processors and the continued ramp of AMD Instinct GPU shipments.'

findings.statement: AMD Q1 2026 non-GAAP gross margin of 55%
findings.why: Solid, stable margins indicate pricing discipline as the Instinct mix ramps.

impact.mechanism: Healthy gross margin reflects pricing power and favorable mix amid the GPU ramp.

evidence.source: AMD Q1 2026 financial results (AMD Investor Relations)
evidence.excerpt: GAAP gross margin 53%; non-GAAP gross margin 55%.

findings.statement: AMD guides Q2 2026 revenue to ~$11.2 billion with non-GAAP gross margin ~56%
findings.why: Sequential guidance up implies continued demand strength and margin expansion into Q2.

impact.mechanism: Raised forward revenue guidance signals sustained demand momentum.

evidence.source: AMD Q1 2026 financial results (AMD Investor Relations)
evidence.excerpt: 'AMD expects revenue to be approximately $11.2 billion, plus or minus $300 million' and 'Non-GAAP gross margin is expected to be approximately 56%.'

findings.statement: Lambda on-demand H100 SXM listed at $3.99/GPU-hour
findings.why: A first-party cloud list price for H100 is a direct read on rental clearing levels for NVIDIA merchant GPUs.

impact.mechanism: On-demand rental price proxies live demand for NVIDIA H100 capacity.

evidence.source: Lambda GPU Cloud pricing (official)
evidence.excerpt: 'NVIDIA H100 SXM: $3.99'

findings.statement: Lambda on-demand B200 SXM6 listed at $6.69/GPU-hour
findings.why: The Blackwell premium over H100 reflects strong demand for the newest NVIDIA merchant GPUs.

impact.mechanism: Premium rental price on newest flagship reflects demand for latest NVIDIA capacity.

evidence.source: Lambda GPU Cloud pricing (official)
evidence.excerpt: 'NVIDIA B200 SXM6: $6.69'

findings.statement: NVIDIA Q1 FY2027 record revenue of $81.6 billion, up 85% year-over-year and 20% sequentially
findings.why: Record top-line growth confirms exceptional and still-accelerating demand for NVIDIA compute.

impact.mechanism: Record revenue momentum evidences sustained merchant GPU demand.

evidence.source: NVIDIA Q1 FY2027 press release (NVIDIA Newsroom)
evidence.excerpt: 'Record revenue of $81.6 billion, up 20% from the previous quarter and up 85% from a year ago.'

findings.statement: NVIDIA Data Center revenue a record $75.2 billion, up 92% year-over-year
findings.why: Data Center is the core merchant-GPU demand signal and it is growing fastest.

impact.mechanism: Data Center revenue growth directly measures merchant GPU demand.

evidence.source: NVIDIA Q1 FY2027 press release (NVIDIA Newsroom)
evidence.excerpt: 'First-quarter revenue was a record $75.2 billion, up 21% from the previous quarter and up 92% from a year ago.'

findings.statement: NVIDIA GAAP gross margin of 74.9% (non-GAAP 75.0%)
findings.why: Very high, stable margins evidence pricing power and tight merchant-GPU supply.

impact.mechanism: Elevated gross margin reflects pricing power under constrained GPU supply.

evidence.source: NVIDIA Q1 FY2027 press release (NVIDIA Newsroom)
evidence.excerpt: 'For the quarter, GAAP and non-GAAP gross margins were 74.9% and 75.0%, respectively.'

findings.statement: NVIDIA Data Center revenue $75.2 billion, up 92% YoY, driven by the ramp of Blackwell 300
findings.why: The Blackwell 300 ramp is the current merchant-GPU demand engine.

impact.mechanism: Blackwell 300 ramp drives Data Center merchant GPU demand growth.

evidence.source: NVIDIA 10-Q Q1 FY2027 (period ended April 26, 2026)
evidence.excerpt: 'Data Center revenue was $75.2 billion, up 92% from a year ago and up 21% sequentially, driven by the ramp of our Blackwell 300 products and demand for our InfiniBand, Spectrum-X Ethernet, and NVLink solutions.

findings.statement: Revenue concentration rising: three direct customers were 21%, 17% and 16% of total revenue
findings.why: Deepening customer concentration is a structural demand risk if any single hyperscaler pulls back.

impact.mechanism: High single-customer concentration raises the demand-cliff risk for merchant GPUs.

evidence.source: NVIDIA 10-Q Q1 FY2027 (period ended April 26, 2026)
evidence.excerpt: 'For the first quarter of fiscal year 2027, three direct customers represented 21%, 17%, and 16% of total revenue'

findings.statement: NVIDIA multi-year cloud service agreement commitments of $30 billion underpin forward demand
findings.why: Contracted multi-year commitments provide visibility into durable forward merchant-GPU demand.

impact.mechanism: Contracted multi-year commitments backstop future merchant GPU demand.

evidence.source: NVIDIA 10-Q Q1 FY2027 (period ended April 26, 2026)
evidence.excerpt: 'Multi-year cloud service agreement commitments as of April 26, 2026, were $30 billion'

findings.statement: NVIDIA effectively foreclosed from China: zero Data Center Hopper shipments vs $4.6B a year earlier
findings.why: Loss of the China data-center market removes a previously material demand pool for NVIDIA merchant GPUs.

impact.mechanism: Export-control foreclosure from China erases a prior merchant-GPU demand channel.

evidence.source: NVIDIA 10-Q Q1 FY2027 (period ended April 26, 2026)
evidence.excerpt: 'No shipments of Data Center Hopper products to China occurred during the quarter, compared with $4.6 billion in the first quarter of fiscal year 2026.'

findings.statement: NVIDIA AI GPU share estimated ~75% in 2026, down from a ~87% peak in 2024
findings.why: NVIDIA's incumbent share is eroding as AMD and custom silicon gain, a structural shift in the merchant-GPU market.

impact.mechanism: Declining NVIDIA share signals relative demand erosion for the incumbent merchant-GPU supplier.

evidence.source: Silicon Analysts - AMD vs NVIDIA AI GPU market share 2026 (secondary estimate)

findings.statement: AMD AI GPU share estimated ~7-10% in 2026, up from ~5% in 2024
findings.why: AMD is steadily gaining merchant-GPU share, validating the second-source thesis.

impact.mechanism: Rising AMD share reflects growing demand for its merchant GPUs.

evidence.source: Silicon Analysts - AMD vs NVIDIA AI GPU market share 2026 (secondary estimate)

findings.statement: Analysts flag hyperscaler custom silicon as a larger threat to NVIDIA than AMD
findings.why: If custom silicon is the bigger structural threat, merchant-GPU demand faces substitution beyond AMD competition.

impact.mechanism: Custom silicon substitution is the dominant structural headwind to merchant GPU demand.

evidence.source: Silicon Analysts - AMD vs NVIDIA AI GPU market share 2026 (secondary estimate)
evidence.excerpt: Article notes hyperscaler custom silicon (Google TPU, AWS Trainium, Broadcom ASICs) as a larger threat to NVIDIA than AMD.

findings.statement: Vast.ai marketplace H100 SXM rents at ~$2.00/GPU-hour
findings.why: Marketplace H100 pricing well below first-party clouds reflects competitive rental supply for NVIDIA GPUs.

impact.mechanism: Low marketplace rental price still reflects active demand for H100 capacity.

evidence.source: Vast.ai pricing (official)

findings.statement: Interruptible/spot H100 capacity on Vast.ai as low as $0.34/GPU-hour
findings.why: Deeply discounted spot rates indicate ample interruptible marketplace supply of NVIDIA GPUs.

impact.mechanism: Very low spot pricing signals abundant interruptible rental supply.

evidence.source: Vast.ai pricing (official)
evidence.excerpt: interruptible/spot as low as $0.34/hr.

findings.statement: BIS shifts to case-by-case licensing for H200/MI325X-class chips to China, reopening a partial China demand channel
findings.why: Moving from denial to conditional approval restores partial access to the China data-center market for NVIDIA H200-class GPUs.

impact.mechanism: Loosened export licensing reopens a portion of China demand for advanced merchant GPUs.

evidence.source: BIS press release - revises license review policy for semiconductors to China (bis.gov)
evidence.excerpt: BIS will review export license applications for 'the Nvidia H200, AMD MI325X, and similar chips on a case-by-case basis provided certain security requirements are met.'

findings.statement: CoreWeave lists NVIDIA HGX H100 8-GPU node at $49.24/hour on-demand
findings.why: An enterprise cloud on-demand H100 node price is a direct read on NVIDIA merchant-GPU rental demand.

impact.mechanism: On-demand node rental price proxies demand for NVIDIA H100 capacity.

evidence.source: CoreWeave GPU pricing (official)
evidence.excerpt: 'NVIDIA HGX H100' — '$49.24' per hour on-demand (~$6.16/GPU-hr)

findings.statement: CoreWeave lists NVIDIA HGX B200 8-GPU node at $68.80/hour on-demand
findings.why: The Blackwell node premium reflects demand for the newest NVIDIA merchant GPUs.

impact.mechanism: Premium node rental price on newest flagship reflects demand for latest NVIDIA capacity.

evidence.source: CoreWeave GPU pricing (official)
evidence.excerpt: 'NVIDIA HGX B200' — '$68.80' per hour (~$8.60/GPU-hr)

findings.statement: Anthropic commits to up to one million TPUs (>=1 GW), a major merchant-GPU substitution
findings.why: A million-chip, gigawatt-scale TPU commitment by a frontier lab proves custom silicon viability and diverts demand from merchant GPUs.

impact.mechanism: Large-scale TPU adoption substitutes for merchant GPU demand.

evidence.source: Google TPU roadmap vs NVIDIA (Data Center Frontier)
evidence.excerpt: Anthropic commitment 'up to one million TPUs, representing >=1 GW of capacity and tens of billions of dollars'

findings.statement: Market bifurcating into GPU-first vs multi-accelerator camps, reducing NVIDIA dependence
findings.why: A structural split where major buyers adopt multiple accelerators lowers dependence on NVIDIA's supply-constrained roadmap.

impact.mechanism: Multi-accelerator procurement leverage reduces captive merchant GPU demand.

evidence.source: Google TPU roadmap vs NVIDIA (Data Center Frontier)
evidence.excerpt: With TPUs proving viable at million-chip scale, large cloud providers now have meaningful leverage in accelerator procurement, reducing dependence on Nvidia's supply-constrained roadmap.

findings.statement: AWS claims Trainium3 saves up to 50% on cost versus GPU training
findings.why: A large cost advantage for a custom ASIC pulls price-sensitive training demand away from merchant GPUs.

impact.mechanism: Cheaper custom-ASIC training substitutes for merchant GPU demand.

evidence.source: AWS Trainium3 launch (Data Center Knowledge)
evidence.excerpt: Cost: 'companies can save up to 50% on costs compared to GPU training.'

findings.statement: Trainium3 delivers up to 362 FP8 PFLOPs, a datacenter-scale merchant-GPU alternative
findings.why: Competitive raw performance establishes Trainium3 as a viable substitute accelerator at scale.

impact.mechanism: High-performance custom silicon erodes the performance moat of merchant GPUs.

evidence.source: AWS Trainium3 launch (Data Center Knowledge)
evidence.excerpt: 'Trainium3 can deliver up to 362 FP8 PFLOPs'

findings.statement: NVIDIA guides Q2 FY2027 revenue to $91.0 billion (+/-2%), excluding any China Data Center compute
findings.why: Guidance well above the current record quarter implies continued strong merchant-GPU demand even with China excluded.

impact.mechanism: Forward revenue guidance signals sustained merchant GPU demand momentum.

evidence.source: NVIDIA Q1 FY2027 press release - Q2 outlook (GlobeNewswire)
evidence.excerpt: 'Revenue is expected to be $91.0 billion, plus or minus 2%.' 'NVIDIA is not assuming any Data Center compute revenue from China in its outlook.'

findings.statement: NVIDIA guides Q2 FY2027 non-GAAP gross margin to 75.0% (+/-50 bps)
findings.why: Sustained ~75% margin guidance signals continued pricing power and supply tightness.

impact.mechanism: Stable high margin guidance reflects durable pricing power under tight supply.

evidence.source: NVIDIA Q1 FY2027 press release - Q2 outlook (GlobeNewswire)
evidence.excerpt: 'GAAP and non-GAAP gross margins are expected to be 74.9% and 75.0%, respectively, plus or minus 50 basis points.'

findings.statement: Intel Data Center and AI (DCAI) revenue $5.1 billion, up 22% year-over-year
findings.why: DCAI growth shows Intel capturing AI datacenter demand and remaining a competitive supply factor.

impact.mechanism: Intel DCAI growth adds competitive datacenter-AI supply against merchant-GPU incumbents.

evidence.source: Intel Q1 2026 financial results (Intel Investor Relations)
evidence.excerpt: Segment revenue: Data Center and AI (DCAI) $5.1 billion, up 22% YoY

findings.statement: Intel non-GAAP gross margin of 41.0%, up 1.8 points year-over-year
findings.why: Margin recovery supports Intel's ability to keep investing in competitive datacenter/AI silicon.

impact.mechanism: Improving Intel margins sustain its competitive investment in datacenter AI accelerators.

evidence.source: Intel Q1 2026 financial results (Intel Investor Relations)
evidence.excerpt: GAAP gross margin 39.4% (up 2.5 pts YoY); non-GAAP gross margin 41.0% (up 1.8 pts YoY).

findings.statement: Export policy shifts from presumption of denial to case-by-case licensing for H200/MI325X-class chips
findings.why: The move away from denial, subject to a 50% volume cap and testing, partially reopens China demand for advanced merchant GPUs.

impact.mechanism: Conditional licensing partially restores China demand access for advanced merchant GPUs.

evidence.source: Morgan Lewis - BIS revises export review policy for advanced AI chips to China
evidence.excerpt: Policy shift from 'a presumption of denial' to 'case-by-case licensing with strict conditions.'

findings.statement: A parallel 25% tariff applies to advanced semiconductors at the same performance thresholds
findings.why: A 25% import tariff raises the landed cost of licensed China-bound chips, dampening the demand the licensing reopens.

impact.mechanism: Import tariff raises cost and blunts the reopened China demand for merchant GPUs.

evidence.source: Morgan Lewis - BIS revises export review policy for advanced AI chips to China
evidence.excerpt: Parallel: Jan 14, 2026 administration '25% tariff on ... semiconductors with the same performance thresholds.'

findings.statement: RunPod lists H100 SXM at $3.29/GPU-hour
findings.why: A first-party cloud H100 SXM list price is a direct rental-demand read for NVIDIA merchant GPUs.

impact.mechanism: On-demand rental price proxies demand for NVIDIA H100 capacity.

evidence.source: RunPod GPU pricing (official)

findings.statement: RunPod lists B200 at $5.89/GPU-hour
findings.why: The Blackwell premium over H100 reflects demand for the newest NVIDIA merchant GPUs.

impact.mechanism: Premium rental price on newest flagship reflects demand for latest NVIDIA capacity.

evidence.source: RunPod GPU pricing (official)

findings.statement: Advanced-GPU lead times remain extended: H100 SXM5 36-52 weeks, H200 40+ weeks, B200 allocated through H2 2027
findings.why: Long and lengthening lead times evidence acute supply constraint on advanced merchant GPUs.

impact.mechanism: Extended lead times reflect constrained merchant GPU supply.

evidence.source: GPU Shortage 2026 - lead times / bottleneck (Spheron)
evidence.excerpt: 'H100 SXM5: 36-52 weeks' from resellers; 'H200 SXM5: 40+ weeks'; 'B200: Allocated through H2 2027'

findings.statement: Structural bottleneck: TSMC CoWoS packaging and SK Hynix HBM fully allocated through mid-2027
findings.why: The upstream packaging and HBM ceilings cap how fast merchant GPU supply can grow regardless of demand.

impact.mechanism: CoWoS and HBM capacity ceilings structurally constrain merchant GPU supply.

evidence.source: GPU Shortage 2026 - lead times / bottleneck (Spheron)
evidence.excerpt: 'CoWoS packaging capacity at TSMC is fully allocated' and 'fully allocated through at least mid-2027'; 'HBM production from SK Hynix cannot keep pace with demand.'

findings.statement: AMD says MI450 lead-customer forecasts now exceed initial plans, with additional multi-gigawatt opportunities
findings.why: Upward customer forecast revisions and new multi-GW engagements point to strengthening forward Instinct GPU demand.

impact.mechanism: Rising MI450 customer forecasts signal growing merchant GPU demand for AMD.

evidence.source: AMD Q1 2026 Earnings Call transcript (Motley Fool via Globe and Mail)
evidence.excerpt: 'lead customer forecasts now exceeding our initial plans and a growing number of new customers engaging on large-scale deployments, including additional multi-gigawatt opportunities.'

findings.statement: AMD targets tens of billions in annual Data Center AI revenue in 2027 with >80% growth
findings.why: Management's multi-year Data Center AI ambition frames a steep forward demand trajectory for Instinct GPUs.

impact.mechanism: Guided long-term Data Center AI growth implies rising merchant GPU demand.

evidence.source: AMD Q1 2026 Earnings Call transcript (Motley Fool via Globe and Mail)
evidence.excerpt: AMD expects 'tens of billions of dollars in annual Data Center AI revenue in 2027 and to exceed our long-term growth target of greater than 80%.'

findings.statement: AMD guides Q2 2026 revenue to ~$11.2 billion (~46% growth) with gross margin ~56%
findings.why: Sequential and year-over-year guided growth supports continued demand momentum into Q2.

impact.mechanism: Raised forward revenue guidance signals sustained demand momentum.

evidence.source: AMD Q1 2026 Earnings Call transcript (Motley Fool via Globe and Mail)
evidence.excerpt: Q2 guidance: 'approximately $11.2 billion (plus or minus $300 million)' with 'expected 46% growth' and 'sequential expansion of approximately 9%'; 'gross margin at 56%.'

bottleneck.rationale: TSMC CoWoS and SK Hynix HBM are fully allocated through mid-2027 and lead times stretch B200 into H2 2027, hard-capping supply.

competitiveStructure.rationale: AMD is gaining share (~5% to ~7-10%) and undercutting NVIDIA pricing 20-30% while Intel enters with Crescent Island, intensifying rivalry.

moat.rationale: NVIDIA's share has eroded from ~87% to ~75% and analysts flag hyperscaler custom silicon as a bigger threat than AMD, signalling a thinning durable advantage.

momentum.rationale: NVIDIA record $81.6B revenue up 85% YoY with data center up 92%, AMD data center up 57%, and a ~$100B Meta MI450 commitment all point to accelerating demand.

strategicRisk.rationale: Custom-silicon substitution is real (Anthropic ~1M TPUs, Trainium3 up to 50% cheaper) and NVIDIA customer concentration is rising.

unitEconomics.rationale: NVIDIA sustains ~75% gross margin with guidance holding at 75%, and AMD runs a healthy 55%, so vendor economics remain robust.

narrative: Merchant-GPU demand and margins are exceptionally strong, with NVIDIA and AMD both compounding at high double-digit growth. The binding constraint is supply, not demand, as CoWoS and HBM remain fully allocated through mid-2027. Competitive and substitution pressures are building but not yet decisive.

categoryStatus.reason: Demand and vendor economics are very strong, so the single binding constraint is physical supply: CoWoS/HBM allocation through mid-2027 gates how much merchant-GPU can ship.
